$comment_1: Kritical SCX config for Continue.dev extension. Copy to %USERPROFILE%\.continue\config.json (Install-KritScxVsCode.ps1 does this).
$comment_2: Env vars {{env.SCX_API_KEY}} are read at Continue runtime from HKCU. Never commit the resolved value.
$comment_3: SCX endpoint accepts anthropic-version header (verified 2026-07-03 with MiniMax-M2.7 returning valid Anthropic-shape response).

models.title: SCX · MiniMax-M2.7 (default agentic, 192K ctx, ~AUD $0.68/1M in + $3.20/1M out)
models.systemMessage: You are a helpful coding assistant. Prefer concise answers with code diffs. Never invent APIs — verify via docs first.

customCommands.name: explain
customCommands.prompt: Explain the highlighted code in plain English. Structure: (1) what it does, (2) what it depends on, (3) what breaks if a caller misuses it. Cite concrete file:line references.

customCommands.name: krit-refactor
customCommands.prompt: Refactor the highlighted code following Kritical conventions: PascalCase for AL identifiers, kebab-case for JS filenames, kr-<scope>-<element> for CSS classes. Never introduce new abstractions unless they save at least 3 duplicate patterns.

customCommands.name: audit-my-change
customCommands.prompt: Audit the CURRENT git diff against the Kritical HARD RULES from CLAUDE.md (never commit .env / real tokens / secrets; no bulk restore; no supervisor cross-invoke; test-dryrun before live). Emit REFUSED/OK per rule with one-line reason.

$comment_krit_1: Autocompact = Continue's 'compaction' feature — enabled by default. When context nears the model contextLength, Continue summarizes older turns automatically. Disable per-conversation if you need full history via /compact off.
$comment_krit_2: Model picker: click the model name at the bottom of the Continue chat panel to switch. Or /model MiniMax-M2.7 in chat.
$comment_krit_3: Fallback: Continue does NOT auto-failover across models. Path C standalone extension is where load-balance + fallback lands. For now, if SCX 429s: click a different model in picker.
